channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: TikTok ban: 5 essential reads on the case and its concerns
item.description: A three-judge panel of the U.S. Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit on December 6, 2024, upheld a law requiring TikTok’s China-based parent company, ByteDance, to sell the video app by January 19, 2025, or face a nationwide ban on the app.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: ACLU spurns 'flawed and dangerous' ruling on TikTok ban
item.description: The American Civil Liberties Union (ACLU) condemned Friday’s “flawed and dangerous” federal appeals court ruling that upheld the law banning the social media platform TikTok.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Revolutionizing Video Analysis: Meet SMAST, the Next-Gen AI Tool
item.description: University researchers unveil SMAST, an AI-powered video analyzer capable of recognizing complex human actions.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: How 2025’s digital products will evolve
item.description: When most people hear “TikTok features,” there’s usually some eye-rolling involved. But after 17 years of helping companies navigate digital transformation, I’ve noticed something more profound happening.
item.dc:creator: Brad Weber, Fast Company

item.title: Federal appeals court upholds law requiring sale or ban of TikTok in the US
item.description: A federal appeals court panel on Friday unanimously upheld a law that could lead to a ban on TikTok as soon as next month, handing a resounding defeat to the popular social media platform as it fights for its survival in the U.S. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Clarifai previews AI compute orchestration
item.description: AI platform provider Clarifai is publicly previewing compute orchestration for AI workloads that the company says works across any AI model on any compute, at any scale.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Apple is about to add seriously useful tools to Apple Intelligence
item.description: Apple is close to introducing iOS 18.2, a major update that brings significant additions to Apple Intelligence, its suite of generative AI (genAI) tools. Highlights of this AI-tinged release include the integration of Siri with ChatGPT, along with new writing and imaging tools.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Ultimate guide to ChatGPT, Gemini, Llama, and other genAI chatbots you need right now
item.description: Since OpenAI first released ChatGPT to the public in November 2022, there has been a proliferation of consumer-facing chatbots in the large language model (LLM) race, changing how consumers interact with information. Chatbots are the interface we use to interact with LLMs.
item.dc:creator: Janya Sundar, Fast Company

item.title: The EU makes an urgent TikTok inquiry on Russia's role in Romanian election turmoil
item.description: The European Union has sent TikTok an urgent request for more information about Romanian intelligence files suggesting that Moscow coordinated influencers on its platform to promote an election candidate who became the surprise front-runner in the nation’s presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What is TypeScript? Strongly typed JavaScript
item.description: Originally developed by Microsoft as a way to adapt JavaScript as a tool to build enterprise-scale applications, TypeScript has become a widely popular programming language that combines JavaScript syntax with strong typing and object-oriented programming.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: China's ban on key high-tech materials could have broad impact on industries, economy
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China has banned exports of key materials used to make a wide range of products, including smartphones, electric vehicles, radar systems and CT scanners, swiping back at Washington after it expanded export controls to include dozens of Chinese companies that make equipment used to pro
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Michigan Democrats move to protect reproductive health data before GOP takes control of House
item.description: Democrats in Michigan are pressing to pass reproductive health care legislation before the party loses its majority with the new legislative session next year.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Volmert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: One Identity Named Winner of the Coveted Top InfoSec Innovator Awards for 2024
item.description: One Identity named Hot Company: Privileged Access Management (PAM) in 12th Cyber Defense Magazine’s Annual InfoSec Awards during CyberDefenseCon 2024.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: These satellites are on a mission to create the first fake solar eclipses. Here’s how
item.description: A pair of European satellites rocketed into orbit Thursday on the first mission to create artificial solar eclipses through fancy formation flying in space. Each fake eclipse should last six hours once operations begin next year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: How the world can tackle the power demands of artificial intelligence
item.description: The world must reshape its technology infrastructure to ensure artificial intelligence makes good on its potential as a transformative moment in digital innovation.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The power of unified data storage with generative AI
item.description: The promise of generative AI (genAI) is undeniable, but the volume and complexity of the data involved pose significant challenges.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Berkeley changes zoning to encourage growth in innovation sector
item.description: Councilmembers voted Tuesday to permit labs studying biosafety levels 2 in mixed-use light industrial zoning areas while prohibiting BSL 3 and 4 uses in all parts of the city.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Lopez, The Mercury News

item.title: From outsider to the Oval Office, bitcoin surges as a new administration embraces crypto
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Born from the ashes of the 2008 global financial crisis, bitcoin arrived after trust had withered in the financial system and in Washington's ability to protect those who must participate in it.
item.dc:creator: STAN CHOE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Musk meets with Thune at the Capitol
item.description: Elon Musk, the co-head of President-elect Trump’s new Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), met with incoming Senate Majority Leader John Thune (R-S.D.) Thursday morning ahead of meetings later in the day with Senate and House Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, The Hill

item.title: Chinese online retailer Temu suspended in Vietnam
item.description: HANOI, Vietnam (AP) — Vietnam has suspended the operations of Chinese online retailer Temu after it failed to meet a government deadline to register the company by the end of November.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Meta to build $10 billion AI data center in Louisiana as Elon Musk expands his Tennessee AI facility
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The largest artificial intelligence data center ever built by Facebook’s parent company Meta is coming to northeast Louisiana, the company said Wednesday, bringing hopes that the $10 billion facility will transform an economically neglected corner of the state. Republican Gov.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK and ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: White House says at least 8 US telecom firms, dozens of nations impacted by China hacking campaign
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A top White House official on Wednesday said at least eight U.S. telecom firms and dozens of nations have been impacted by a Chinese hacking campaign.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Meta quietly leans on rival GPT-4 despite Zuckerberg’s bold Llama claims
item.description: Mark Zuckerberg has consistently championed Meta’s Llama AI model as a leader in generative AI technology, positioning it as a strong competitor to OpenAI and Google. However, behind the scenes, Meta is complementing Llama with a rival AI model to meet its internal needs.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Kotlin previews guard conditions in when expressions
item.description: JetBrains has released Kotlin 2.1.0, an update to the Kotlin programming language that previews guard conditions and has compiler updates offering flexibility around checks. Released November 27, Kotlin 2.1.0 can be installed as an update by following the instructions at kotlinlang.org. Kotlin 2.1.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Federal Executive Forum Profiles in Excellence in Government: CIO Achievements 2024
item.description: Join host Luke McCormack as he discusses 2024 federal IT successes with top government and industry technology experts. The post Federal Executive Forum Profiles in Excellence in Government: CIO Achievements 2024 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: TikTok defends handling of Romania election content in grilling by EU lawmakers
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — TikTok took down several networks that tried to meddle in Romania's elections, executives said Tuesday as they defended the company's election integrity measures to European Union lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN and STEPHEN McGRATH, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: AT&T sees earnings growth over next 3 years; eyes more than $40B in anticipated shareholder returns
item.description: AT&T anticipates earnings growth over the next three years thanks to the momentum of 5G and fiber services.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN, Associated Press, KRON4
